subject: fix(audit): pane z-index symmetry, font-sans override, Date filter perf
body:
#1 — SettingsPane backdrop z-index asymmetry + misleading comment
  DetailPane backdrop was z-30, SettingsPane backdrop was z-40, both
  panes were z-40. The settings backdrop being at the SAME level as
  the detail aside (z-40) only worked via DOM source order — the
  comment falsely claimed "Higher z-index so it stacks above detail."
  Future readers would see the equal z-index and either "fix" it
  (breaking the stacking) or be confused by the lying comment.

  Unified both panes to use the same pair: z-30 backdrop + z-40 aside.
  Stacking still works via DOM source order (settings markup comes
  after detail, so at equal z-index the later-painted wins). Comment
  rewrote to describe the actual mechanism instead of the wrong
  z-index claim.

#2 — `font-sans` Tailwind utility overrode global Figtree font
  App.jsx:348 and EmbedRenderer.jsx:160 had `font-sans` on their
  embed/error fallback divs. Tailwind's font-sans = `font-family:
  ui-sans-serif, system-ui, ...` (system stack), which has higher
  CSS specificity than the global `* { font-family: var(--font-sans) }`
  rule that sets Figtree. Result: error messages rendered in the OS
  system font instead of the project's Figtree, visually inconsistent
  with the rest of the dashboard.

  Dropped `font-sans` from both fallbacks. They now inherit Figtree
  from the global `*` rule like every other element.

#4 — Date construction inside filterCommits filter callback
  The dateTo branch did:
    if (filters.dateTo) {
      const toDate = new Date(filters.dateTo);
      toDate.setHours(23, 59, 59, 999);
      const commitDate = new Date(commit.timestamp);
      if (commitDate > toDate) return false;
    }
  Two `new Date()` constructions per commit (toDate + commitDate),
  plus a setHours mutation, plus a Date comparison. For a 10k-commit
  dataset that's 20k+ Date allocations per filter operation.

  Hoisted both date bounds out of the .filter() callback into the
  function body, converted to `getTime()` numeric millisecond
  comparison. Per-commit overhead drops from 2 Date constructions +
  Date comparison to 1 Date construction + numeric comparison.
  Total filter overhead: O(2N) → O(N+2) Date allocations.

  Also removed the local Date mutation — toDate.setHours() is now
  called once at the top, on the single hoisted instance, not a
  side-effect inside what should be a pure filter callback.

Verified: vite build clean, 60/60 tests pass.

Tags: fix, perf, accessibility, ui
Complexity: 2
Urgency: 2
Impact: user-facing
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: vanilla-daisyui-audit
Semver: patch
